hooks.statusMessage: Checking for upstream changes before push…
hooks.command: input=$(cat); cmd=$(printf '%s' "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); printf '%s' "$cmd" | grep -qE '(^|[;&|]|&&|[[:space:]])git[[:space:]]+push' || exit 0; git fetch --quiet 2>/dev/null; behind=$(git rev-list --count HEAD..@{u} 2>/dev/null); if [ "${behind:-0}" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo "PUSH BLOCKED: your branch is $behind commit(s) behind $(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name @{u}). Another session/push landed first. Review what changed with:  git log HEAD..@{u} --oneline  — then integrate (git pull --rebase origin main), rebuild & re-verify in the browser, and push again." >&2; exit 2; fi
